metadata.seed: No. So since then, I've been on really just high alert. Like, I'm worried that that could happen to me, to my kids, and I'm just really worried because they didn't catch the guy, that him or someone like him could be out there.

patient.query: No. So since then, I've been on really just high alert. Like, I'm worried that that could happen to me, to my kids, and I'm just really worried because they didn't catch the guy, that him or someone like him could be out there.

automatic_thoughts: I could be in danger
my kids could be at risk
the criminal is still out there

something happening to them

it's probably fine

patient.query: It’s a mix of things, honestly. I start thinking about how much I care about them and how I would never forgive myself if something happened. Then, it just blows up in my head, and I feel like I’m stuck in this loop. I just end up feeling really anxious and... kind of frozen, I guess. It's exhausting.

I would never forgive myself if something happened
It blows up in my head
I feel stuck in this loop

patient.query: When it blows up, it’s like this avalanche of thoughts. I go from just a small worry to imagining all these terrible situations, like accidents or sickness. My heart starts racing, and I feel this heavy weight in my chest. It’s hard to breathe sometimes, and I just keep thinking about all the ways things could go wrong. Everything feels so real, even though I know it’s all in my head.

automatic_thoughts: imagining terrible situations
thinking about all the ways things could go wrong

patient.query: It really does feel overwhelming. It’s like my body is on high alert, but I don’t even know what I’m supposed to be on alert for. I just wish I could turn it off, but it’s like it has a mind of its own. And then, I feel guilty for feeling this way, like I’m not being strong enough for them.

patient.query: Well, I think it comes from my own childhood. My parents had their own struggles, and I often felt like I had to be the one to keep things together. So, when I feel this way, it just brings back that sense of needing to be strong, like if I show any cracks, it’s going to fall apart. I don’t want to put that kind of pressure on them, you know? I just... don’t want to repeat that cycle.
